summary:
Phase 15 — Super Admin Account regression. Backend: 19/20 in /app/backend/tests/test_phase15_account.py PASS. Covered: admin overview shape + 10% platform-take math, MRR chart 6-month chronological, top-hosts ranking + platform_take per host, transactions filter + sort desc, CSV export Content-Type/Disposition + amount_inr column, mock-pay writes booking_payment ledger + idempotent on retry (no dup), admin refund full tier, admin refund override 75%, refund dup-guard 400, guest cancel auto-refund (refund + ledger written), payout sweep marks eligible (gross 500000/fee 50000/net 450000 paise for ₹5k booking), payout fail when destination missing, batch process-eligible math (processed+failed=total, idempotent 0 on second run), host payout-pref validation (bare upi 400, bare bank 400, valid UPI 200, valid bank 200 returns ******7890 mask), host /payouts list filtered to own host_id with property enrichment, registration_fee ledger backfilled rows, and unique-index regression on payouts.booking_id + payouts.payout_id (DuplicateKeyError on conflict). Phase 12-14 regression suites all green (27/27).

1 BACKEND BUG FOUND in process_payout (admin-initiated payout). 500 with stack 'str object has no attribute value' — see RCA below.

Frontend: AdminAccount /admin/account loads via nav-account-btn from admin dashboard; all 8 stat tiles render with non-empty INR amounts (gross ₹14,04,984; platform-take ₹1,40,198 = gross*10% verified; bookings ₹14,01,984 / 45 bookings; reg fees ₹3,000 / 6 hosts; subs ₹0; refunds ₹0). MRR chart-card renders recharts SVG. Tabs Transactions/Payouts/Refunds wire up correctly (transactions-table, filter-type, export-csv-btn, payouts-empty visible when none, open-refund-modal opens refund-modal). Top-hosts tab opens after dismissing refund modal. Host /host/payouts page renders payout-destination form with UPI/bank radios, masked '******7890' next to current account, Save preference button. ZERO console errors except a benign recharts width(-1) warning on first paint.

FRONTEND GAP: HostDashboard does NOT render data-testid=nav-payouts-btn (review_request asserts it should). /host/payouts route works via direct URL but host has no entry-point in dashboard nav.

critical.issue: 500 'str object has no attribute value' when host has a saved payout_preference. Root cause: services/account_service.py:298 dest_type = pref.get('preferred', payout.destination_type) returns the raw string from Mongo ('upi'); line 331 assigns this string to payout.destination_type (Pydantic v2 validate_assignment is OFF so no coercion); line 352 then calls payout.destination_type.value → AttributeError. Confirmed in /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log. FIX: cast at read — dest_type = PayoutDestinationType(pref.get('preferred', payout.destination_type)) — or change line 352 to f'{payout.destination_type if isinstance(payout.destination_type,str) else payout.destination_type.value}'. Also a serialization warning on db.payouts.update_one({'$set': payout.model_dump()}) for the same reason. This blocks single-payout admin processing in the success path; batch process-eligible silently masks it (counts as failed).

ui_bugs.issue: Missing data-testid=nav-payouts-btn — host has no nav entry to /host/payouts from dashboard. Direct URL still works.

issues: Recharts logs width(-1) height(-1) warning before card sizes — benign but spammy in console

action_items: FIX (CRITICAL): /app/backend/services/account_service.py — cast dest_type to PayoutDestinationType enum after pref.get(), so payout.destination_type.value works at line 352. One-liner: `dest_type = PayoutDestinationType(pref.get('preferred', payout.destination_type.value if isinstance(payout.destination_type, PayoutDestinationType) else payout.destination_type))`.
ADD nav-payouts-btn to HostDashboard.js header (parallel to nav-account-btn on AdminDashboard).

(Older infra, not Phase 15) test_phase6/7/8/9/10/11 files lack `load_dotenv('/app/frontend/.env')` so they collection-error with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL='' when invoked outside the project root. Two-line fix per file.
critical_code_review_comments: account_service.process_payout — payout.destination_type is mutated mid-flow with raw str from mongo dict. Use Pydantic enum coercion or model_validate. Same risk in mark_booking_payout_eligible:267 if pref dict contains canonical strings.

account_service.initiate_refund: razorpay_service.is_mock is captured TWICE (one for Refund.is_mock, again in record_transaction). If is_mock toggles between calls it could desync — use a single local var.

admin_account_routes.process_eligible silently swallows exceptions in `except Exception: failed += 1`; this is what hid the destination_type bug from the batch path. Add logger.exception to the except so future bugs surface.

admin_account_routes.create_refund only blocks dup if booking_status=='cancelled' AND refund_status=='processed'. A refund initiated on an active confirmed booking does NOT mark booking_status=cancelled (only refund_status), so calling the endpoint twice on the same un-cancelled booking will create TWO Refund rows + two ledger entries. Test_admin_refund_dup_guard had to manually flip booking_status=cancelled to trigger the guard. Strengthen the guard to also reject when refund_status in ('processed','pending').

_compute_mrr divides plan price (rupees) * 100 / duration_months. For yearly plans this loses sub-paise precision but is fine. For trial subs that are counted alongside active — confirm trial subs should contribute to MRR (currently they do; usually they don't).
transactions index lacks (host_id, type, status) compound — top-hosts aggregation will table-scan as ledger grows; consider adding type_1_status_1_host_1 for the aggregation path.

host_account_routes.update_payout_preference returns the unmasked bank_account_number in payout_preference echo (only adds masked alongside). Consider stripping bank_account_number from the response payload.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_phase15_account.py (new — 20 tests, 19 passing, 1 surfacing real backend bug)

success_rate.frontend: Admin Account 100%, Host Payouts 90% (missing nav button)

test_credentials: All 5 demo accounts working — admin/host/broker/employee/guest as per /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: Phase15 tests create ephemeral TEST_p15 properties + bk_TEST_p15_<8hex> bookings + TEST_idx payout rows; module-level autouse fixture deletes all of them on teardown (bookings, payouts, refunds, transactions filtered by booking_id IN cleanup set). Host's payout_preference is left as the last-saved valid bank info (1234567890 / HDFC0000123) — main agent may want to reset.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes account_service.py:298+ (cast preferred to enum), re-run `pytest tests/test_phase15_account.py::test_process_payout_with_upi -v` to confirm 20/20. Don't re-run test_unique_indexes_payouts in isolation — it needs autouse cleanup. Booking-create returns flat shape: keys=[booking_id, razorpay_order_id, razorpay_key_id, amount, currency, booking_details]; total is at body['booking_details']['total_amount'] in rupees. Backfilled ledger snapshot: 45 booking_payment txns (₹14,01,984 gross), 6 registration_fee (₹3,000 — host plus 5 seed-artefact non-hosts), 0 subscription, 0 refund, 0 payout (until tests ran). Razorpay mock prefixes: pout_mock_ for payouts, rfnd_mock_ for refunds, order_mock_ + pay_mock_ for bookings.
rca of the issue: PAYOUT 500 — account_service.process_payout reads pref['preferred'] (a raw str post mongo round-trip) and assigns it to payout.destination_type which is typed as PayoutDestinationType enum. Pydantic v2 doesn't coerce on assignment (validate_assignment=False default). Subsequent f-string at line 352 calls .value on the str → AttributeError → 500. Reproduction: PUT /api/host/payout-preference {preferred:'upi', upi_vpa:'host@upi'} as host, sweep eligibility, then POST /api/admin/account/payouts/{id}/process as admin. Mitigation (one-liner in services/account_service.py:298): replace `dest_type = pref.get('preferred', payout.destination_type)` with `_pref_str = pref.get('preferred'); dest_type = PayoutDestinationType(_pref_str) if _pref_str else payout.destination_type`. Also wrap line 331 with isinstance check or import + cast.
